desc: Tyre is one of the Levant's great archaeological coast cities, with Roman streets, a huge hippodrome, a smaller old harbour and long sandy shoreline all tied to an extraordinary Phoenician history. Under normal conditions it would be an easy archaeology-and-sea destination, but practical planning is currently dominated by South Lebanon security conditions rather than classic holiday logic.

todo.name: Al Mina archaeological site
todo.desc: Walk Roman streets and harbour-side ruins that reveal Tyre's long Mediterranean role.

todo.name: Al Bass archaeological site
todo.desc: Visit the necropolis, triumphal arch and hippodrome for the city's monumental Roman layer.

todo.name: Tyre Coast Nature Reserve
todo.desc: Use the protected beach area for dunes, nesting-season awareness and a less built-up coast.

todo.name: Old harbour dinner
todo.desc: Finish with seafood around the small harbour when conditions and opening hours line up.

todo.name: Harbour quarter walk
todo.desc: Use the tighter lanes around the fishing harbour for a feel of the older city beyond the headline archaeological compounds.

todo.name: Sandy public beach edge
todo.desc: If local conditions are calm and current advice allows, the open shoreline shows why Tyre has long mattered as more than a ruins stop.

wifi.note: Usable in central guesthouses and some seafront cafes, but outages, generator dependence and weaker consistency outside the core mean you should not plan on seamless always-on connectivity.

prac.fltNote: Tyre has no airport and no destination-level LGW/LCY nonstop routing. Beirut is the only practical air gateway, and Beirut airport's own distance guide puts Tyre at roughly 77 to 83 km away, but the key issue is not distance: FCDO currently advises against all travel to South Governorate, which includes Tyre. If someone is already making an essential trip, they should plan around confirmed flights, current road conditions and trusted local logistics rather than assume a routine coastal transfer.
prac.currency: Lebanese pound (LBP); US dollars are also widely used in some travel contexts
prac.lang: Arabic; French and English are common in Beirut and tourism services
prac.visa: For British citizens who choose to travel against FCDO advice, GOV.UK says a free single-entry tourist or family visit visa can normally be obtained on arrival at Beirut airport, your passport should be valid for at least 3 months from entry, and travellers with Israeli stamps may be refused entry.
alerts: FCDO currently advises against all travel to South Governorate, which includes Tyre.
If your presence in Lebanon is not essential, GOV.UK says you should consider departing now if you judge you can do so safely.
Road access, checkpoints and local conditions can change quickly.
If travel is essential, use trusted local drivers and keep plans flexible to same-day disruption.

sweet: In pure weather terms May, June and October would usually be the most comfortable months, but current security advice should outweigh seasonality when planning Tyre at all.
